title: card::extract_first_url returns Option<String> when Option<&str> suffices
description:
LOCATION: src/card.rs:19-24

FINDING: extract_first_url returns Option<String> via m.as_str().to_string(). The match lives for the regex lifetime which is 'static (LazyLock), so the underlying &str borrow from find() is valid only for the lifetime of html. The function signature could return Option<&str> with lifetime tied to the input, saving the allocation. All three call sites (cli.rs:274, webhook.rs:110 via spawn_fetch, feed.rs:137 via spawn_fetch) immediately clone the result anyway when passing to spawn_fetch, but cli.rs uses it directly.

IMPACT: One String allocation per post that contains a URL. Minor, but the signature communicates false ownership semantics.
